facts.text: Lealman was founded in the late 19th century by Elza Beasley Lealman Sr., a Georgia native who turned a rural outpost into a thriving agricultural community.
The town’s name honors Elza Lealman, who served as Suwannee County sheriff during the Civil War before moving to Pinellas in the 1870s.
Lealman Heights, the area’s original name, grew around a train station established by 1895, linking the community to the CSX Railroad.
Since 2016, Lealman has been a Community Redevelopment Area, receiving tax‑increment financing for parks, crosswalks, and affordable housing projects like Greenway Lofts.
The neighborhood’s median home value sits around $175,000, with owner‑occupied units making up roughly 63% of the housing stock.
Lealman’s elevation is 49 feet above sea level, placing it comfortably above the region’s typical floodplain.
